rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective three-component alkylarylation of acrylates with aliphatic C(sp3)-H (alkanes) and aryl halides via asymmetric paired oxidative (Fe/LMCT photocatalysis) and reductive (chiral Ni) electrocatalysis in an undivided cell
electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes Fe(II) to Fe(III) to regenerate [FeCl4]- which under light undergoes LMCT to give Cl radical (HAT agent); cathode reduces Ni(II) precatalyst to Ni(I) to initiate the Ni cross-coupling cycle and reduces Ni(III)-alkyl back to Ni(I) after reductive elimination

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical from HAT on alkane (species E, e.g. cyclohexyl)
alpha-ester carbon radical after Giese addition to acrylate (species F)
LnNi(III)-Ar (species H) from oxidative addition of Ar-X
LnNi(III)(aryl)(alkyl) species J after radical capture

electron_flow: At the anode, Fe(II) is oxidized to Fe(III) generating [FeCl4]- which is photoexcited and undergoes LMCT to release a Cl radical that abstracts H from the alkane to give an alkyl radical; this radical adds to the acrylate, and the resulting alpha-ester radical is captured by a chiral aryl-Ni(II) species (formed by cathodic reduction of Ni(II) and oxidative addition of Ar-X to Ni(I)) to give Ni(III)(aryl)(alkyl), which reductively eliminates the product and the Ni(I) catalyst is regenerated/refreshed by cathodic reduction.
rate_determining_step: Electron transfer at the electrodes (reaction is first-order in current)

probe_reagents.name: 1-methylnaphthalene (no Ni, radical trap by chlorination)
probe_reagents.observation: 1-(chloromethyl)naphthalene 29 isolated in 27% yield, indicating Cl-radical mediated HAT generates a benzylic radical that is chlorinated

probe_reagents.name: chlorocyclohexane (replacing alkane)
probe_reagents.observation: no desired product formed, excluding alkyl chloride as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: chlorocycloheptane + cyclohexane competition
probe_reagents.observation: only cyclohexane-derived product 1 observed, alkyl chloride is not an intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Ac-C6H4-Ni(II)-Br stoichiometric complex
probe_reagents.observation: with cyclohexane, tert-butyl acrylate and FeCl3 under light gave product 1 in 31% yield, 91% ee — supports aryl-Ni(II) as productive intermediate

probe_reagents.name: L1 with varying ee (nonlinear effect study)
probe_reagents.observation: positive nonlinear effect — at least two chiral BiIm ligands involved in the enantio-determining step

probe_reagents.name: ee vs time monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: ee of product 1 constant throughout reaction — stereo-convergent, not deracemization

probe_reagents.name: current variation kinetics
probe_reagents.observation: first-order dependence of rate on current (R^2=0.985), confirming electron transfer is rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: electrode potential monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: anode held ~+0.28 V vs Ag/AgCl (Fe(II)/Fe(III)); cathode ~-0.83 V vs Ag/AgCl, sufficient to reduce ArNi(III) to ArNi(II) but not ArNi(II) to ArNi(I)

notes: Mechanism is a dual Fe/Ni paired electrocatalytic cycle: anodic Fe-LMCT photocatalysis activates the C(sp3)-H bond of the alkane (HAT by Cl•), while cathodic chiral Ni catalysis controls enantioselective C(sp3)-C(sp2) bond formation. Positive NLE suggests aggregation of >=2 chiral BiIm ligands on Ni in the stereo-determining step. Alternative pathway via alkyl chloride intermediates was explicitly ruled out by competition and substitution experiments. Single-crystal X-ray confirmed absolute configuration of product 7.
